texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded official Idaho education pass across the four remaining district-owned hosts plus their current machine-readable inventory surfaces. Jefferson remains positively recovered from district-owned `special-education`, `special-services`, `section-504`, and `student-services` leaves. Oneida remains positively recovered from the district-owned Child Find PDF. But the residual four districts now finalize as wrong-role survivors rather than unknown roots. Camas still only exposes a district-owned `Contact Information` leaf with district address and phone, while both `robots.txt` and `sitemap.xml` 404; the apparent raw `504` token on Camas resolves only to numeric payload noise, not a role-bearing Section 504 leaf. Clark exposes a real public XML sitemap at `https://www.clarkcountyschools161.org/fs/pages/sitemap` plus exact district-owned `Contact Us` and `Title IX` leaves, but the sitemap inventory still yields no `special-education`, `special-services`, `student-services`, `section-504`, `child-find`, or procedural-safeguards page. Fremont exposes a live sitemap at `https://www.sd215.net/sitemap.xml`, and additional bounded checks on `https://www.sd215.net/documents` plus `https://www.sd215.net/page/district-forms` also stay generic; none of those surfaces yields a role-bearing special-education or child-find leaf, and direct bounded probes for `/page/special-education`, `/page/special-services`, `/page/student-services`, `/page/section-504`, and `/page/child-find` all 404. Shoshone exposes live WordPress post sitemaps, but those feeds still only materialize generic district, board, agenda, and newsletter pages, and the taxonomy sitemap guess 404s; direct bounded probes for `/special-education/`, `/student-services/`, `/special-services/`, `/section-504/`, and `/child-find/` all 404. Idaho therefore remains blocked because the remaining district-owned roots, document lanes, and live sitemap inventories still materialize only wrong-role or generic leaves rather than local special-education routing.

Reviewed 2026-06-23 bounded live Idaho DHW confirmation on the official root plus the existing office-leaf packet. The public root at https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/offices is live with title `Find a Service Location` and still preserves exact city office cards like Caldwell Office in HTML, but it exposes zero county terms or county-served fields. The exact office leaf https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/dhw/caldwell-office is live with title `Caldwell Office`, confirming that the packet is grounded in real reviewable DHW office leaves. Idaho county-local routing therefore splits cleanly into 17 safe county-to-exact-office replacements plus one Canyon split that still rejects Nampa as SWITC-only, while 27 counties remain blocked because the public DHW stack still exposes no truthful county-to-office contract for them.
